title: Fanout Exchanges and True Publish/Subscribe · Type-System Trace: Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility
summary: Audit, notification, search, and analytics consumers share one queue and steal work from each other; a fanout exchange exists but late or disconnected subscriber behavior is undocumented. Planned artifact: Add and verify the subscriber queue per responsibility increment in fanout subscription topology and lifecycle map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy.
Acknowledge only after a durable effect or safely repeatable handoff and retain message identity, attempt, result, and failure evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility · Theory Subscriber Queue Per R…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 6671 in a regional incident workflow during one broker-node restart. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove one subscription queue per responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy.
